Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The largest acidic geyser has been putting on quite a show 53 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. Graphics Programming Resources 106 upvotes, 12 comments
  3. An Interactive Intro to CRDTs (2023) 153 upvotes, 23 comments
  4. Textadept 144 upvotes, 25 comments
  5. Show HN: I made a zero-copy coroutine tracer to find my scheduler's lost wakeups 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. On the Design of Programming Languages (1974) [pdf] 47 upvotes, 8 comments
  7. Motorola GrapheneOS devices will be bootloader unlockable/relockable 769 upvotes, 248 comments
  8. Voxile: A ray-traced game made in its own engine and programming language 205 upvotes, 58 comments
  9. Launch HN: Cekura (YC F24) – Testing and monitoring for voice and chat AI agents 84 upvotes, 20 comments
  10. Speculative Speculative Decoding (SSD) 46 upvotes, 9 comments
  11. Better JIT for Postgres 71 upvotes, 18 comments
  12. Claude's Cycles [pdf] 650 upvotes, 271 comments
  13. Agentic Engineering Patterns 176 upvotes, 77 comments
  14. A CPU that runs entirely on GPU 116 upvotes, 48 comments
  15. RE#: how we built the fastest regex engine in F# 19 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. You can use newline characters in URLs 85 upvotes, 36 comments
  17. RFC 9849. TLS Encrypted Client Hello 93 upvotes, 42 comments
  18. Indefinite Book Club Hiatus 39 upvotes, 18 comments
  19. Number Research Inc 40 upvotes, 19 comments
  20. Show HN: Stacked Game of Life 23 upvotes, 9 comments
  21. Don't become an engineering manager 363 upvotes, 259 comments
  22. Reverse-Engineering the Wetware: Spiking Networks and the End of Matrix Math 24 upvotes, 10 comments
  23. Weave – A language aware merge algorithm based on entities 130 upvotes, 85 comments
  24. GPT‑5.3 Instant 359 upvotes, 277 comments
  25. TikTok will not introduce end-to-end encryption, saying it makes users less safe 266 upvotes, 221 comments
  26. Intel's make-or-break 18A process node debuts for data center with 288-core Xeon 288 upvotes, 251 comments
  27. Welcoming Elizabeth Barron as the New Executive Director of the PHP Foundation 31 upvotes, 20 comments
  28. MacBook Pro with M5 Pro and M5 Max 792 upvotes, 840 comments
  29. When AI writes the software, who verifies it? 244 upvotes, 242 comments
  30. My spicy take on vibe coding for PMs 118 upvotes, 110 comments
